faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Bay Ridge home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool clothing or blankets, silken tubes or cases in closets, fine webbing on fabric surfaces, and small beige or gold-colored moths flying around your closets or bedroom. You may also notice larvae (small white caterpillars) on affected items. Our Bay Ridge technicians provide thorough inspections to identify the extent of infestation.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control cost in Bay Ridge?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in Bay Ridge typically ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of infestation, size of the affected area, and number of treatments required. A single-room treatment starts around $550, while whole-home infestations in larger Bay Ridge residences may require comprehensive treatment plans. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take in Bay Ridge homes?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically takes 2-4 hours depending on your home's size and infestation severity. Most Bay Ridge clothes moth cases require 2-3 follow-up treatments spaced 2-3 weeks apart to target newly hatched larvae and break the reproductive cycle. Our technicians work efficiently to minimize disruption to your daily routine while ensuring complete elimination.

faqs.question: Are your clothes moth treatments safe for Bay Ridge families and pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products and integrated pest management techniques that are safe for families and pets when applied correctly. Our Bay Ridge technicians provide clear instructions for any necessary preparation, such as temporary relocation of pets during treatment. We prioritize low-toxicity solutions and focus on targeted application to protect your clothing while keeping your household safe.
